THAN THE PARTIES ARE TO APPEAR IN 302 TO ADDRESS THE MOTION. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: 49 ZOE STREET HOMEOWERS'ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. CHARLES O.JONES CO.,INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JUNE 10, 2008 LINE 5, DEFENDANT ZOE ASSOCIATES LLC, CHARLES O. JONES COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE COMPLAINT IS BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS CCP 337.15. THE COMPLAINT WAS FILED WHEN PLAINTIFF'S CORPORATE STATUS WAS SUSPENDED AND REMAINED SUSPENDED WHEN THE LIMITATIONS PERIOD EXPIRED. THIS IS SUBSTANTIAL DEFENSE AND THE REVIVAL OF THE CORPORATE STATUS IS NOT ENTITLED TO RETOACTIVE APPLICATION. BENTON V. COUNRTY OF NAPA (1991) 226 CAL. APP 3RD 1485, 1490. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: CACH, LLC VS. REGINALD STEPHENSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Vacating Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JUNE 10, 2008 LINE 8, PLAINTIFF CACH, LLC'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION For Order Vacating Dismissal IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED NO EVIDENCE OF DILIGENCE IN MOVING FOR RELIEF UNDER CCP 473(B) BILLINGS V. HEALTH PLAN OF AMERICA (1990) 225 CAL. APP. 3RD 250, 254-255. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF'S DISMISSAL WAS VOLUNTARY RATHER THAN A RESULT OF MISTAKE, SURPRISE, INADVERTANCE OR NEGLECT. CCP 473(B) DOES NOT APPLY TP PLAINTIFF'S VOLUNTARY ACT IN DISMISSING THE CASE. =(302/PJM/X-AM)
